overall_summary: The ElizaOS community is currently navigating a period of significant tension characterized by sharp declines in token value and project communication concerns, while simultaneously seeing high-level B2B architectural proposals and expansion into enterprise integrations like WhatsApp and Gmail.
key_facts: Jaime Vejar Aguirre proposed a B2B AI agent architecture for YOYO utilizing LangGraph, MCP, and Supabase with pgvector.
ElizaOS plugin for ZARQ crypto risk intelligence was published, providing pre-trade scoring for 205 tokens.
The project confirmed active multi-chain operations currently limited to Solana and BSC (Binance Smart Chain).
Developers reported persistent model configuration issues across different agents and requested a Google voice plugin to replace ElevenLabs.
Project leadership confirmed ongoing development of the 'milady project' and the 'Spartan Degen AI' component.
Documentation and code examples for implementing timed agent-to-agent Discord interactions were provided via the milady-ai and elizaos/examples repositories.
Integrations for WhatsApp, Gmail, and N8N workflow engine reached final stages of implementation in the core repository.
The SAID Protocol was introduced to provide agents with verifiable on-chain identities on the Solana blockchain.
open_questions: Is the team interested in OTC investment?
Why doesn't the team implement buybacks during periods of price depression?
Why does Shaw hold 2.6% of the token supply?
Who has built an adaptor for elizaOS and what are the experiences in terms of distribution?

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor
new_issues_prs.author: unavailable
new_issues_prs.significance: Aims to improve long-term system architecture and reliability.

new_issues_prs.title: SAID Protocol integration for on-chain Solana identity
new_issues_prs.author: unavailable
new_issues_prs.significance: Enables verifiable agent signatures and digital passports.

new_issues_prs.title: Embedding failures on Linux environments
new_issues_prs.author: community-reported
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical blocker for Linux users utilizing Ollama for embeddings.

overall_focus.claim: Focus shifted toward a major beta release, prioritizing platform stability, messaging app integrations (WhatsApp/Gmail), and decentralized identity tools.

discord_updates.summary: Heavy focus on token price FUD and project management feedback balanced by high-level B2B hiring and architectural pitches.
Technical troubleshooting regarding agent-to-agent communication timers and cost-efficient voice plugin alternatives.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed frustration over delayed game/app launches, unclear airdrop plans, and insufficient social media presence to counter market FUD.
Developer request for a native Google Voice plugin to avoid high costs associated with ElevenLabs integration.

strategic_insights.insight: There is emerging developer interest in using ElizaOS for autonomous B2B purchasing and ERP integration, moving beyond simple social agents.
implications_or_questions: Should the framework prioritize enterprise-grade connectors?
How does the core team view computer-use capabilities for ERPs?

strategic_insights.insight: Community leaders acknowledge that fair launch models suffer from a lack of operational treasury, potentially hindering long-term marketing and development.
implications_or_questions: How can the project fund a 'responsible marketing team' as requested by users?

market_analysis.observation: ELIZAOS token reached new all-time lows, leading to community anxiety and accusations of team disinterest.
market_analysis.relevance: Directly impacts community morale and perceived project legitimacy during the beta transition.
